CREATE TABLE ip_addresses (id INT PRIMARY KEY, ip_address VARCHAR(50));
CREATE TABLE network_info (net_id INT PRIMARY KEY, net_address VARCHAR(50), description TEXT);
INSERT INTO network_info VALUES (1, '10.0.0.1/8', 'Corporate network'), (2, '172.16.0.1/12', 'Private network');
SELECT inet(net_address) FROM network_info WHERE net_id = 1;
INSERT INTO ip_addresses SELECT net_id, inet(net_address) FROM network_info;
CREATE TABLE logs (log_id INT PRIMARY KEY, ip_address INET, timestamp TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO logs (log_id, ip_address, timestamp) SELECT 1, inet(ip_address), CURRENT_TIMESTAMP FROM ip_addresses;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM logs WHERE ip_address = inet('192.168.1.5/24');
SELECT a.id, inet(a.ip_address) AS inet_ip, b.description FROM ip_addresses a INNER JOIN network_info b ON inet(a.ip_address) = inet(b.net_address);
WITH ip_data AS (SELECT inet(ip_address) AS valid_ip FROM ip_addresses) SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ip_data WHERE valid_ip IS NOT NULL;
SELECT inet('255.255.255.255') AS max_inet, inet('0.0.0.0') AS min_inet;
CREATE TABLE test_cases (case_id INT PRIMARY KEY, case_description TEXT);
INSERT INTO test_cases VALUES (1, 'Boundary test for maximum inet address'), (2, 'Boundary test for minimum inet address');
SELECT CASE WHEN inet('255.255.255.255') IS NOT NULL THEN 'Valid' ELSE 'Invalid' END AS test_result FROM test_cases WHERE case_id = 1;
SELECT CASE WHEN inet('256.100.50.25') IS NULL THEN 'Invalid' ELSE 'Valid' END AS test_result FROM test_cases WHERE case_id = 2;
